title: bridelia patula
intro: Bridelia patula, also known as the Croton plant, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark, leaves, and roots exhibiting medicinal properties. The bark of Bridelia patula contains a compound called limonoids, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain. Bridelia patula can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
